English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Thomas Lyson Papers
Lyson, Thomas A.
21-33-3600
5.4 cubic feet. 5.4 cubic feet.
1971-2007. Publications, Research Materials, Correspondence Includes correspondence, course material, and publications concerning Lyson's work. Collection material in English 6062681
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Anne Eller
Date completed:
July 2007
EAD encoding:
Anne Eller, July 2007
Date modified:
RMC Staff, October 2011
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor of Development Sociology, Cornell University from 1987-2007.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes correspondence, course material, photographs, and publications concerning Lyson's work on community, civic engagement and problem-solving approaches to development, as well as local and global aspects of agriculture, food, nutrition, and health. Papers and examinations from his years as a doctoral student at Michigan State and files and a manuscript relating to academic job searches in the late 1970s.
Cite As:
Thomas Lyson papers, #21-33-3600. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Photographs. Land use, Rural -- United States. Rural development -- United States. Farms -- United States. Agriculture -- Economic aspects -- United States. Agriculture -- Social aspects -- United States. Cornell University -- : Faculty. New York State College of Agriculture and Life Sciences. Department of Development Sociology
